# Multi-Head Attention — Many Conversations at Once {#sec-ch07-multi-head-attention}

> _"One head hears syntax. Another hears coreference. A third hears topic. They all speak simultaneously."_

A single attention head is expressive, but limited.
It produces one set of attention weights — one pattern of "who attends to whom."
In natural language, multiple distinct relationships coexist in the same sentence.
Consider:

```text
"The animal didn't cross the street because it was too tired."
```

- One pattern might link "it" → "animal" (coreference)
- Another might link "cross" → "street" (verb-object)
- Another might track the causal structure "because"

Multi-head attention runs `H` independent attention heads in parallel, each free to specialize on a different relationship type.
Their outputs are concatenated and projected back to the model dimension.

{{< include book/07-multi-head-attention/sections/idea.qmd >}}

{{< include book/07-multi-head-attention/sections/math.qmd >}}

{{< include book/07-multi-head-attention/sections/matrix.qmd >}}

{{< include book/07-multi-head-attention/sections/code.qmd >}}

{{< include book/07-multi-head-attention/sections/takeaways.qmd >}}
